title: [InstCombine] Canonicalize Bit Testing by Shifting to Bit 0

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions i32 examples but doesn't explicitly cover i64 width; existing known #3 uses i32, this tests the optimization applies to 64-bit integers

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @test_const_lshr_eq_zero_i64(i64 %b) {
#0:
  %shr = lshr i64 127, %b
  %and = and i64 %shr, 1
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %and, 0
  ret i1 %cmp
}
=>
define i1 @test_const_lshr_eq_zero_i64(i64 %b) {
#0:
  %cmp = icmp ugt i64 %b, 6
  ret i1 %cmp
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions 'exact flags' in testing considerations but no existing test case uses the 'exact' keyword on the shift instruction

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @test_const_lshr_exact_flag(i32 %b) {
#0:
  %shr = lshr exact i32 255, %b
  %and = and i32 %shr, 1
  %cmp = icmp ne i32 %and, 0
  ret i1 %cmp
}
=>
define i1 @test_const_lshr_exact_flag(i32 %b) {
#0:
  ret i1 1
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary and existing tests focus on icmp ne pattern; this tests icmp eq with 0 which is semantically equivalent but uses different comparison predicate

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @test_const_lshr_eq_zero_different_constant(i32 %b) {
#0:
  %shr = lshr i32 1024, %b
  %and = and i32 %shr, 1
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %and, 0
  ret i1 %cmp
}
=>
define i1 @test_const_lshr_eq_zero_different_constant(i32 %b) {
#0:
  %#1 = icmp ne i32 %b, 10
  ret i1 %#1
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors
